sketchBlocks.title: Understanding Boolean Logic
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown: Boolean logic is fundamental in programming, allowing us to combine conditions using operators like AND, OR, and NOT.

quizDraft.title: Check Eligibility for a Discount
quizDraft.prompt: Write a program that checks if a customer is eligible for a discount based on their membership status and purchase amount.
quizDraft.hint: Consider both membership status and purchase amount in your conditions.

help.concept: You can use boolean operators to combine multiple conditions. For example, use AND to check if both conditions are true.
help.hint_1: Think about how to structure your if statements to check both conditions.
help.hint_2: Remember to use the correct logical operators to combine your conditions.

quizDraft.starterCode:
membership = input('Enter membership status (gold/silver/none): ')
purchase_amount = float(input('Enter purchase amount: '))

# Your code here

quizDraft.title: Determine Voting Eligibility
quizDraft.prompt: Create a program that checks if a person is eligible to vote based on their age and citizenship status.
quizDraft.hint: You need to check both age and citizenship.

help.concept: Eligibility can be determined by combining conditions with AND. Both conditions must be true for eligibility.
help.hint_1: Consider how to structure your if statement to check both age and citizenship.
help.hint_2: Make sure to use the correct logical operator to combine your checks.

quizDraft.starterCode:
age = int(input('Enter your age: '))
citizenship = input('Are you a citizen? (yes/no): ')

# Your code here

quizDraft.title: Understanding AND Operator
quizDraft.prompt: What does the AND operator do in boolean logic?
quizDraft.hint: It requires both conditions to be true.

help.concept: The AND operator combines two conditions and returns true only if both conditions are true.
help.hint_1: Think about how AND works in everyday logic.
help.hint_2: If one condition is false, the entire expression is false.

options.text: Returns true if at least one condition is true.
Returns true only if both conditions are true.
Returns false if both conditions are true.

quizDraft.title: Identifying Boolean Operators
quizDraft.prompt: Which of the following are boolean operators?
quizDraft.hint: Look for operators that combine conditions.

help.concept: Boolean operators are used to combine conditions in logical expressions.
help.hint_1: Common boolean operators include AND, OR, and NOT.
help.hint_2: Consider which of the options can be used to combine logical statements.

quizDraft.title: Complete the Logic Statement
quizDraft.prompt: Fill in the blank: The expression 'x > 5 _____ y < 10' will evaluate to true if both conditions are true.
quizDraft.hint: Think about how to combine conditions.

help.concept: You can use boolean operators to combine conditions in logical expressions.
help.hint_1: Consider which operator requires both conditions to be true.
help.hint_2: This operator is commonly used in programming.

quizDraft.template: The expression 'x > 5 {blank} y < 10' will evaluate to true if both conditions are true.
